bullets.summary: Nvidia Q4 FY22 (ended Jan 30 2022): record total revenue of $7.64B (+53% YoY); data center revenue a record $3.26B (+71% YoY, +11% QoQ); Q1 FY23 guidance of $8.10B (+/- 2%). Demand continues to exceed supply.
Nvidia Q1 FY23 revenue tracking above $5.30B outlook, with all market platforms driving upside. Company expects demand to continue to exceed supply for much of the year.
Alphabet Q4 2021 earnings (reported Feb 1 2022): Google Cloud revenue +45% YoY to $5.54B; cloud backlog grew 70%+ to $51B; 65% YoY growth in cloud deals worth over $1B.
Alphabet total Q4 2021 revenue +32% YoY; YouTube and Search ad revenue both beat estimates; management characterizes cloud opportunity as 'very early innings'.
Microsoft Q2 FY22 (ended Dec 2021, reported Jan 25 2022): Azure and cloud services revenue +46% YoY; overall cloud revenue $22.1B (+32% YoY); EPS $2.48 adjusted vs $2.31 expected.
Microsoft Intelligent Cloud segment revenue increased 29% driven by Azure; commercial cloud revenue surpassed $22B quarterly run-rate for the first time.

Apple Q1 FY22 (ended Dec 2021, reported Jan 27 2022): all-time record quarterly revenue of $123.9B (+11% YoY), beating $118.3B estimate; iPhone revenue $71.6B (+9% YoY); Services $19.5B (+24% YoY); 785M paid subscribers.
Apple CEO Tim Cook guided for solid YoY revenue growth in the March quarter, with supply constraints expected to be less severe than the December quarter.

S&P 500 returned -4.6% in Q1 2022; the 10-year Treasury yield rose from 1.51% to 2.32% over the quarter; the Fed delivered its first rate hike since 2018 in March 2022 and signaled six more hikes for the year.

Markets staged a relief rally after the March 16 Fed rate hike as investors found comfort in the rate-hike path being clarified; S&P 500 snapped a three-day losing streak, bouncing off correction lows.
AGG (iShares Core U.S. Aggregate Bond ETF) lost approximately 5.9% in Q1 2022, its worst quarterly return in decades, as the Fed began raising rates and the 10-year yield surged from 1.51% to 2.32%.
bullets.source: TCV Trust & Wealth Management Q1 2022 Review

bullets.summary: BIL (SPDR Bloomberg 1-3 Month T-Bill ETF) holds ultra-short Treasuries with minimal duration; as of Q1 2022, yields were near zero and just beginning to rise with the Fed's first rate hike in March 2022; the fund is effectively a cash proxy.

Gold jumped 6% in February 2022 on safe-haven demand tied to Russia's invasion of Ukraine; LBMA Gold Price PM ended February at $1,910/oz. Gold reached a peak of $1,973.96 in early March 2022, its highest since September 2020.
After peaking near $1,974 in early March 2022, gold retreated as the Fed's March rate hike lifted real yields; the metal remained above $1,900 but gave back roughly half its war-premium.
Broad Q1 2022 tech/growth selloff hit thematic ETFs hard; the Nasdaq-100 fell 8.9% in Q1 2022 and smaller speculative growth names and thematic funds experienced steeper losses than large-cap tech.

IBM unveiled its 127-qubit Eagle quantum processor at the IBM Quantum Summit on November 16, 2021, described as the first processor whose scale makes classical simulation infeasible; IBM roadmap targets 433-qubit Osprey in 2022 and 1,121-qubit Condor in 2023.
IBM Eagle qubit coherence time reached 300 microseconds by early 2022 — roughly triple the median for superconducting qubits; performance benchmarks presented at APS March Meeting 2022.

VIG (Vanguard Dividend Appreciation ETF) focuses on companies with multi-year dividend growth records; in the Q1 2022 risk-off environment driven by Fed rate hikes and Ukraine war, quality/dividend-growth strategies generally held up better than growth-heavy names.

NVDA.rationale: Q4 FY22 data center revenue hit a record $3.26B (+71% YoY) on Feb 16 2022, with Q1 FY23 guidance of $8.10B and demand continuing to exceed supply, indicating compounding adoption with clear revenue proof.

GOOGL.rationale: Q4 2021 earnings (reported Feb 1 2022) showed Google Cloud +45% YoY to $5.54B and backlog growing 70%+ to $51B, with management framing cloud as 'early innings', consistent with compounding adoption.

MSFT.rationale: Q2 FY22 results (reported Jan 25 2022) showed Azure +46% YoY and commercial cloud surpassing a $22B quarterly run-rate for the first time, with EPS beating estimates.

AAPL.rationale: Q1 FY22 results (reported Jan 27 2022) showed all-time record quarterly revenue of $123.9B beating estimates across all major product categories, with Services growing 24% YoY to $19.5B and 785M paid subscribers.

SPY.rationale: SPY is a general-markets broad index ETF; Q1 2022 returned -4.6% on macro headwinds (Fed rate hikes, Ukraine war) with no wave-specific signal.

AGG.rationale: AGG is a broad investment-grade bond ETF in the general_markets bucket; its -5.9% Q1 2022 loss is a macro rate-rising event with no wave classification applicable.

BIL.rationale: BIL is an ultra-short T-bill ETF in the general_markets bucket; no wave signal and minimal per-ticker news materialized from searches.

IAU.rationale: IAU is a precious metals ETF in the general_markets bucket; the Ukraine safe-haven move is a macro geopolitical event, not a wave-cycle classification.

BOTZ.rationale: No BOTZ-specific dated news article was found from before 2022-04-01; the only available bullet covers the broad Nasdaq/growth-ETF selloff in Q1 2022, which is not BOTZ-specific.

QTUM.rationale: Downgraded from buildup: the IBM Eagle and coherence-time bullets document the quantum sector's research progress but are not QTUM-specific (no QTUM fund news, flows, or company-level event found before 2022-04-01); per the grounding rule, a non-neutral call requires a ticker-specific bullet.

VIG.rationale: VIG is a dividend-appreciation ETF in general_markets; the single sourced bullet is a general description rather than a dated event specific to VIG.

self_critique_downgrades.reason: IBM Eagle and coherence benchmarks are quantum-sector milestones, not QTUM-fund-specific events. The grounding rule requires at least one bullet specific to the ticker (QTUM) to support a non-neutral call; no such QTUM-specific dated article was retrieved before 2022-04-01.
